title: Gooey Peppery Ukrainian Vegetable Stock Borscht
description: A hearty, comforting dish from Ukraine featuring a rich vegetable stock with a gooey, peppery finish. Perfect for a solo dinner after a long day.
instructions: Heat the oil in a cataplana over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the carrots and beetroot, and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the broth, vinegar, thyme, parsley, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Return the stock to the cataplana and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the liquid has thickened and reduced slightly, about 10 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Note: This recipe makes about 6 servings, so feel free to adjust the ingredient quantities as needed.
Note: If using a store-bought broth, you may want to add a teaspoon of tomato paste to give it a richer flavor.
